businessModel.text: Revenue from franchise royalties (45% of systemwide sales), company-operated restaurant sales (35%), and Other (Prima, McPlant, delivery commissions, ~20%). 93% of global restaurants are franchisee-owned.

recentCatalysts.text: Mobile app and loyalty programme: 37M US members, 25% of systemwide sales CosMc's small-format brand launched 2024 - targeting daypart expansion Alliance Partner platform (Uber Eats, Google, Tiktok) expanding international reach US menu simplification: 30% fewer SKUs → kitchen throughput improvement
items: Mobile app and loyalty programme: 37M US members, 25% of systemwide sales
CosMc's small-format brand launched 2024 - targeting daypart expansion
Alliance Partner platform (Uber Eats, Google, Tiktok) expanding international reach
US menu simplification: 30% fewer SKUs → kitchen throughput improvement

keyRisks.text: 1. US comp-sales turn negative in Q3 FY25 - traffic remains below pre-COVID levels 2. $5 meal deal was commercially necessary but structurally damages brand equity 3. Commodity and labour inflation: restaurant margin 18.2% vs 21.5% pre-inflation era 4. Wages: US median hourly rate up 20% since 2021 - structural cost headwind 5. Tariffs on imported produce and packaging (~$900M exposure)

risks.risk: 1. US comp-sales turn negative in Q3 FY25 - traffic remains below pre-COVID levels
2. $5 meal deal was commercially necessary but structurally damages brand equity
3. Commodity and labour inflation: restaurant margin 18.2% vs 21.5% pre-inflation era
4. Wages: US median hourly rate up 20% since 2021 - structural cost headwind
5. Tariffs on imported produce and packaging (~$900M exposure)

whoShouldOwn.text: Defensive consumer-staples trade for balanced portfolios. Strong brand and global franchise provide FCF floor. NOT suitable for growth investors; turnaround narrative requires patience and execution proof.

entryExit.text: Build position $295-$310. HOLD $310-$328. REDUCE above $328. Track: US comparable sales and monthly traffic data. Key inflection: Q4 FY25 comp normalisation. | BUY: Build position $295-$310. | REDUCE above $328.

sources.marketData: Live quote data sourced via the DYOR HQ proprietary market data workflow using multiple market data inputs and validation checks. Fields include price, market capitalisation, 52-week range, P/E, EPS, and volume.
sources.companyFilings: Public company filings, regulatory announcements, investor presentations, and regulatory announcements where referenced in the analysis.
